varieties.name: White Star
varieties.traits: Determinate type, medium to large oval tubers, white skin, shallow eyes
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, floury texture, dry when cooked
varieties.yield_notes: High yield potential under winter conditions
varieties.growing_notes: TOP CHOICE for Brisbane subtropics. Determinate growth means single layer tubers, no hilling required. Performs excellently planted July-August. Widely recommended by subtropical gardeners. Good disease resistance.
varieties.availability: Australian seed potato suppliers, Brisbane nurseries

varieties.name: Sebago
varieties.origin: USA (Maine), 1938. Cross of Chippewa × Katahdin. Arrived Victoria 1940
varieties.traits: Long to oval shape, creamy-white skin, shallow eyes, uniform size
varieties.growing_notes: Australia's most popular variety. Excellent all-rounder for subtropics. Originally developed for late blight resistance. Suitable for boiling, mashing, roasting, baking, chips. Very versatile and widely available. Good storage life.
varieties.availability: All Australian supermarkets and nurseries, certified seed widely available

varieties.name: Desiree
varieties.traits: Oval shape, smooth pink/red skin, fleet eyes
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent all-rounder for Brisbane. Suitable for roasting, mashing, baking. Holds shape well. One of Australia's favorite potatoes. Performs well in subtropical conditions. Good for multiple uses.
varieties.availability: Widely available Australian supermarkets, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Kennebec
varieties.traits: Large, shallow eyes, white skin, tubers form high on stem

varieties.name: Coliban
varieties.traits: Medium to late season, round to oval, white skin
varieties.growing_notes: Australian-bred variety. Good all-rounder for mashing, baking, roasting, French fries. Widely grown commercially. Suitable for subtropical growing. Available fresh markets.
varieties.availability: Australian supermarkets, commercial seed suppliers

varieties.name: Pink Eye (Tasmanian Pink Eye)
varieties.origin: Australia (Tasmania), dating back to 1880s-1940s. Heirloom variety
varieties.traits: Round to oval, cream/mauve skin with distinctive pink/purple eyes. Determinate type
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT heritage variety for subtropics. Tasmania's favorite potato. Fast-growing determinate type (10-13 weeks). Perfect for boiling, baking, salads. Holds shape well. Popular variety across Australia. Good for new potatoes. Available certified seed.
varieties.availability: Tassie Seed Potatoes, Australian nurseries, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Kipfler
varieties.origin: Austria (heritage variety). Known as German Finger Potato or Austrian Crescent
varieties.traits: Fingerling type, long cigar-shaped, light brown to dusty yellow skin. Determinate
varieties.growing_notes: One of Australia's most popular fingerling varieties. Nutty, buttery flavor. Excellent for salads, roasting, boiling, steaming. Fast growing determinate (10-13 weeks) suitable for subtropics. Holds shape when cooked. Highly valued by chefs.
varieties.availability: Australian farmers markets, specialty nurseries, Brisbane suppliers

varieties.name: Dutch Cream
varieties.traits: Smooth pale yellow skin, shallow eyes, oval shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rich yellow, waxy, firm, creamy texture

varieties.name: Nicola
varieties.traits: Oval shaped, beige-yellow skin, cream colored
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect all-rounder with sweet nutty flavor. Excellent for mashing, gnocchi, boiling, baking, salads, roasting. Indeterminate type. Very versatile. Popular in Australian markets.
varieties.availability: Australian supermarkets, Brisbane markets, nurseries

varieties.name: Pontiac (Red Pontiac)
varieties.traits: Round shape, bright red thin skin, deep eyes. Determinate
varieties.growing_notes: Popular waxy red potato. Excellent for boiling, roasting, baking, salads. Doesn't darken after cooking. Determinate fast grower (10-13 weeks). Good for subtropical conditions. Small commercial production in Australia.
varieties.availability: Australian seed suppliers, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Russet Burbank
varieties.origin: USA, 1850s-1906. Descendant of Early Rose variety developed by Luther Burbank
varieties.traits: Large, elongated, rough dark brown russet skin, deep eyes
varieties.growing_notes: World's #1 French fry potato. Australia's restaurant standard for wedges. Heritage variety. Excellent for baking, chips. Most popular variety in North America. Can grow in subtropics but prefers cooler conditions. Commercial variety in Tasmania.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Australian specialty suppliers, limited supermarket availability

varieties.name: Spunta
varieties.traits: Large oval shape, smooth yellow skin, shallow eyes. Determinate
varieties.growing_notes: Versatile all-rounder. Good for boiling, baking, mashing, microwaving, deep-frying. Determinate fast grower (10-13 weeks) ideal for subtropics. Widely grown commercially in Queensland.
varieties.availability: Australian markets, Queensland commercial suppliers

varieties.name: King Edward
varieties.traits: Cream skin with pink markings, smooth
varieties.growing_notes: Classic British heritage potato. Gorgeous masher and fluffy roaster. Good for baking. Indeterminate type. Can be grown in subtropics with care. Available from heritage suppliers.
varieties.availability: Diggers Club, heritage potato suppliers, Australian specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Otway Red
varieties.traits: Smooth skinned, dark red skin
varieties.growing_notes: Australian variety. Excellent mashed, roasted, fried. Grown in SE Queensland with success. Some sources report red varieties can struggle in warmer climates, but Otway Red adapted.
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries, Queensland suppliers

varieties.name: Bintje
varieties.traits: Smooth pale yellow skin, shallow eyes, mid-season (90-110 days)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow, waxy firm, fine creamy texture
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, widely adaptable, stores well
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for salads, frying, boiling, roasting. Long shelf life. Dutch heritage variety introduced to Australia in 1990s. Gourmet variety. Good adaptability.
varieties.availability: Australian specialty markets, gourmet suppliers

varieties.name: Pink Fir Apple
varieties.traits: Unusual long knobby banana/cigar shape, pink skin
varieties.growing_notes: Classic heirloom fingerling. Excellent for salads, roasting, boiling, baking. Too dry for roasting alone. Holds shape well. Available Australian nurseries. Indeterminate type.
varieties.availability: The Lost Seed, heritage suppliers, Australian specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Royal Blue
varieties.traits: Blue/purple skin
varieties.growing_notes: All-rounder variety. Introduced to Australia 1990s. Boosted fresh market. Good general purpose potato.
varieties.availability: Australian specialty markets

varieties.name: Bismarck
varieties.origin: Germany, introduced to Tasmania 1880s. Historic Australian variety
varieties.traits: Early maturing, long oval, creamy white tubers, purple eyes
varieties.growing_notes: Historic variety grown in Tasmania/north-west Australia since 1880s. Ideal for boiling as new potato. Makes great chips when mature. Crispy potato skins. Popular in early 1900s.
varieties.availability: Heritage Fruits Society, Tasmanian suppliers, heritage collections

varieties.name: Purple Congo
varieties.traits: Purple skin, determinate type
varieties.growing_notes: Specialty purple variety. Steam, boil, or mash. Too dry for roasting. Floury type. Determinate fast grower suitable for subtropics. Unique color.
varieties.availability: Australian specialty nurseries, farmers markets

varieties.name: Atlantic
varieties.traits: Round to oval, white skin
varieties.growing_notes: Main processing variety for crisps in Australia. Grown commercially in Queensland and other states. Good chip-making variety. Available as seed.
varieties.availability: Commercial seed suppliers, some nurseries

varieties.name: Kufri Surya
varieties.origin: India (CPRI, Shimla), 2005. Developed from International Potato Centre, Lima, Peru germplasm
varieties.traits: Early maturing (80-90 days), oblong tubers, white smooth skin

varieties.name: Kufri Lima
varieties.origin: India (CPRI) in collaboration with International Potato Center (CIP)
varieties.traits: Early maturing, heat and disease resistant
varieties.yield_notes: 23.5-36.6 t/ha depending on harvest time. Very high for heat conditions

varieties.name: Kufri Khyati
varieties.traits: Heat tolerant variety
varieties.yield_notes: 30.2 t/ha at 75 days in trials. High yielding

varieties.name: Kufri Jyoti
varieties.traits: Large oval, white skin, fleet eyes, small swift eyes
varieties.growing_notes: Widely cultivated across India including Karnataka (peninsular region near Kerala). Moderately resistant to early and late blight. Suitable for processing. Grown in Bihar, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Haryana, Punjab, Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal. Can adapt to warmer climates.
varieties.availability: Widely available Indian seed suppliers, Kerala agricultural stores

varieties.name: Kufri Chandramukhi
varieties.traits: Large, round to oval, white skin, slightly flattened eyes

varieties.name: Kufri Sindhuri
varieties.origin: India (CPRI), 1966. Cross of Kufri Kundan × Kufri Red
varieties.traits: Medium sized, round, red skin, deep eyes

varieties.name: Kufri Badshah
varieties.origin: India (CPRI), 1980. Cross of Kufri Jyoti × Kufri Alankar
varieties.traits: Large to medium, red, round, medium deep eyes
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, tasty with good flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Released for Indo-gangetic plains and Plateau region of Peninsular India. Moderately resistant to early blight. Resistant to Potato Virus Y (PVY). Tolerant to frost and late blight. Good table quality. Grown in Jammu Kashmir, Haryana, Punjab, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh.
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers, available in south India

varieties.name: Kufri Bahar
varieties.origin: India (CPRI), 1980. Cross of Kufri Red × Ginek
varieties.traits: White, large, round to oval, deep eyes. Slow degeneration rate
varieties.growing_notes: Released for plains of Haryana, Punjab, Western Uttar Pradesh. Very good keeper (10-12% weight loss). Resistant to late blight, early blight, leaf roll. Suitable for diverse culinary applications.
varieties.availability: Northern Indian suppliers, Kerala via dealers

varieties.name: Kufri Pukhraj
varieties.origin: India (CPRI), 1998. Cross of Craig's Defiance × JEX/B-687
varieties.traits: Large, oval, slightly tapered, white skin, fleet eyes
varieties.growing_notes: Widely adaptable variety. Resistant to early blight, moderately resistant to late blight. Suitable for table purposes. Grown in Maharashtra, Orissa, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Gujarat, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Madhya Pradesh, West Bengal. Very good keeper. High water use efficiency.
varieties.availability: Available across India including Kerala region

varieties.name: Kufri Ashoka
varieties.traits: Early variety, large oval-long, white skin, fleet eyes
varieties.growing_notes: Widely adaptable variety. Released to replace Kufri Chandramukhi. Suitable for short day conditions. Grown in Bihar, Haryana, Punjab, Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal. Susceptible to late blight. Table purpose.
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers, Kerala agricultural centers

varieties.name: Kufri Lauvkar
varieties.traits: Large, round, white skin, fleet eyes. Early tuberization in warm climates
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for warm climate regions like Kerala. Able to build yields rapidly under warmer climate. Primarily cultivated in Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, Karnataka. Perfect for processing into chips. Heat-tolerant characteristics.
varieties.availability: Southern India suppliers, Karnataka/Kerala dealers

varieties.name: Kufri Jawahar
varieties.origin: India (CPRI), 1996. Cross of Kufri Neelamani × Kufri Jyoti
varieties.traits: Medium round-oval, creamy white skin, fleet eyes
varieties.growing_notes: Early maturing variety suitable for intensive cropping systems. Moderately resistant to late blight. Grown in Gujarat, Haryana, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Karnataka (southern region). Table purpose variety.
varieties.availability: Available in Karnataka and surrounding southern states including Kerala

varieties.name: Kufri Chipsona-1
varieties.traits: Medium to large, oval, white skin, fleet eyes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dull white flesh, high dry matter (9.8%)
varieties.growing_notes: Primarily for making chips and French fries. Resistant to late blight, tolerant to frost. Grown mainly in Bihar and Uttar Pradesh. Excellent keeper. Higher germination percentage. Highest dry matter production suitable for processing industry.
varieties.availability: Northern India suppliers, available through Kerala dealers

varieties.name: Kufri Chipsona-3
varieties.traits: Less sucrose content
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for making chips. Recommended for Northern Telangana Zone, can be grown in southern regions. Late wilt disease doesn't occur. Good processing quality.
varieties.availability: Processing industry suppliers, southern India dealers

varieties.name: HT/7-1329 (Advanced Heat Tolerant Hybrid)
varieties.origin: India (ICAR-CPRI). Advanced heat tolerant breeding line
varieties.traits: Heat tolerant, good plant vigor
varieties.yield_notes: 23.4-35.6 t/ha depending on harvest time. Outstanding under heat stress

varieties.name: HT/10-2002 (Heat Tolerant Hybrid)
varieties.traits: Heat tolerant characteristics

varieties.name: Kufri Kiran
varieties.traits: Early planting capability (15-20 days earlier than normal)
varieties.yield_notes: 11% higher tuber yield than other heat-tolerant varieties
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for subtropical North Indian plains and plateau regions. Can be planted earlier in season. Suitable for main season in plateau regions. Good heat tolerance characteristics.
varieties.availability: CPRI dealers, northern and central India suppliers

varieties.name: Kufri Pushkar
varieties.traits: White tubers, medium maturing
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent keeper (lowest total weight loss 7.7%). Late wilt disease resistant. Can be stored in normal conditions. Higher germination percentage. Suitable for table purposes. Good marketable yield.
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers nationwide

varieties.name: Chinese Potato (Plectranthus rotundifolius) - Koorka 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Kerala. Known as 'Koorka' in Malayalam
varieties.traits: Small tubers, harder than Irish potato, aromatic
varieties.flesh_fruit: Unique taste and aroma, desired shape
